channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Matt Canavan faces challenge of countering Pauline Hanson and One Nation support
item.description: Matt Canavan, the new leader of the Nationals, is stepping into the political arena to face Pauline Hanson and One Nation, much like his mentor, the late Ron Boswell, did years ago. Boswell considered...

item.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Amid Escalating Conflict with US and Israel
item.description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to U.S.-Israeli strikes, cutting off a major route that carries around one-fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas. Traffic through t...

item.title: Ijebu Pride Network Holds Courtesy Visits to Prominent Business Figures in Ago-Iwoye and Ijebu-Igbo
item.description: The Ijebu Pride Network (IPN), under the leadership of its President, Basorun Dr. Femi Branch, carried out important courtesy visits on Thursday to key business leaders in Ijebuland as part of efforts...

item.title: Conservationists Face Growing Mental Health Challenges Amid Environmental Crises
item.description: The article discusses the rising mental health crisis among conservation scientists who are struggling with despair due to environmental destruction, low wages, poor job security, and demanding worklo...

item.title: Trump and Xi set to hold talks in Beijing as Iran conflict looms
item.description: Just a couple of weeks before President Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing, Chinese officials are cautious due to uncertainty about the US leader’s intentions and the ongoing Iran conflict. This trip, Tr...

item.title: NDMA warns of rain, snowfall increasing landslide and flood risks in northern Pakistan
item.description: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued a cautionary advisory for travellers and residents of northern Pakistan, as rain and snowfall are expected to continue until March 16, 2026...

item.title: Experienced Pilot Reassures Politicians on Helicopter Safety Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: Following the tragic helicopter crash in Nandi County on February 28, which claimed the lives of Emurua Dikirr MP Johana Ngeno and five others, veteran aviator Brigadier Tanui has moved to reassure po...

item.title: Escalating Middle East tensions as US and Iran clash over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have intensified, with the US and Israel striking Iranian military targets on Kharg Island. Despite reports of damage, Iranian state media claims oil exports remain uninter...

item.title: Australia Releases Fuel Reserves Amid Global Supply Disruptions
item.description: Australia is facing significant pressure on its fuel supply due to international tensions in the Middle East and subsequent impacts on global oil prices. The government, led by Energy Minister Chris B...

item.title: Fuel Prices Expected to Rise Again, Drivers Weigh Halting Operations
item.description: Fuel prices in the Philippines are set for another significant increase, prompting concern among public utility and delivery drivers. The anticipated hike comes amid continuing global oil market volat...

item.title: Notorious gang leader killed in broad daylight in Kollam over long-standing feud
item.description: In a shocking incident reflecting the ongoing gang rivalry in Kollam, a known gang leader, Atul, also called Aluva Atul, was brutally hacked to death on Saturday morning. The attack occurred on a busy...

item.title: Shifts in Global Power Strengthen Western Democracies
item.description: The article discusses a perceived shift in the global balance of power in favour of Western democracies. It highlights recent actions by the United States to curb drug trafficking from China and throu...

item.title: Tinubu Says He Reads Newspapers Daily and Urges Media to Monitor All Levels of Government
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don yan Nigerians say him dey very concerned about welfare of ordinary people. E talk say every morning before e commot from him house, e dey always read newspapers to sabi wetin...

item.title: Tanzania and Uganda consider using national IDs to ease travel and trade
item.description: Tanzania and Uganda are currently looking at the option of allowing citizens to use their national ID cards as official travel documents between the two countries. This discussion happened during the ...

item.title: Traditional Beliefs and Practices Against Adultery in Various Nigerian Communities
item.description: This article explore different cultural and spiritual traditions for Nigeria wey dey deal with adultery, especially for married women. E start with recent gist about one woman wey marry man from Oroka...

item.title: Bungoma Goliath Opens Up About Obinna's Advice and His Journey to Success
item.description: Content creator and influencer Bungoma Goliath, whose real name is Isaac Otesa, has shared his emotional journey from rejection to eventual success. During an interview hosted by YouTuber Oga Obinna, ...

item.title: Trump Supporter Laura Loomer Apologises for Controversial Social Media Remarks on India
item.description: American far-right activist and staunch supporter of former US President Donald Trump, Laura Loomer, has issued an apology for some of her past social media posts that were considered offensive to Ind...

item.title: Preparations and Traffic Plans for Kilimanjaro International Marathon in Moshi
item.description: Moshi town is gearing up for the Kilimanjaro International Marathon scheduled for Sunday, 22 March 2026. The organizers have released detailed information about road closures and traffic arrangements ...

item.title: Police investigate death of man found in wheelie bin in Coventry
item.description: West Midlands Police are investigating the death of a man discovered in a wheelie bin in Coventry. The body, thought to belong to a man aged between 40 and 50, was found by a member of the public in a...

item.title: Modernising Agriculture to Boost Tanzania’s Economy and Livelihoods
item.description: Tanzania is moving forward with its National Development Vision 2050, aiming to create a strong, inclusive middle-income economy. Agriculture, which remains crucial for millions of Tanzanians, especia...
